Both use v3 proto types:\n   - SotW: envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest/DiscoveryResponse\n   - Delta: envoy::service::discovery::v3::DeltaDiscoveryRequest/DeltaDiscoveryResponse\n\n2. **Subscription State Management**: Separate implementations for protocol variants:\n   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h/cc handles state-of-the-world protocol sessions\n   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h/cc handles delta protocol sessions\n   - Both inherit from BaseSubscriptionState template (subscription_state.h)\n\n3. **Proto Type Mapping and Selection**: source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.h/cc implements crucial functions that map resource type URLs to appropriate gRPC service methods:\n   - sotwGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects SotW endpoint for resource type\n   - deltaGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects Delta endpoint for resource type\n   - These function as the dual-version subscription handling mechanism, allowing Envoy to select between SotW and Delta protocols for each resource type\n\n4. **Subscription Factory Pattern**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.h/cc and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_collection_subscription_factory.h/cc implement factories that create appropriate subscription types:\n   - GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates SotW subscriptions\n   - DeltaGrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates Delta subscriptions\n   - Selection between legacy mux (GrpcMuxImpl, NewGrpcMuxImpl) and unified mux (XdsMux::GrpcMux variants) controlled by runtime feature flag 'envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux'\n\n5. **Failover Support**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_failover.h provides arbitration between primary and failover config sources, supporting both SotW and Delta protocol variants through template specialization.\n\n6. **Resource Decoding**: source/common/config/decoded_resource_impl.h standardizes resource field access (version, resource, metadata) from v3 proto resources, regardless of protocol variant.\n\n7. **Proto Service Registration**: source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h ensures all v3 proto service descriptors (CDS, EDS, LDS, RDS, SRDS, RTDS, SDS, HDS, LEDS, ECDS) are available in the descriptor pool.\n\n8. **Watch and Resource Management**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h/cc manages resource subscriptions and version tracking, operating across both protocol variants.\n\nThe architecture demonstrates a sophisticated migration strategy where v2-to-v3 transition occurs at the proto definition level (with versioning annotations indicating previous_message_type), while the C++ runtime uses only v3 types. The dual-version subscription handling is achieved through protocol selection mechanisms (SotW vs Delta) rather than v2 vs v3 API versioning. Both protocols are v3-based and provide different bandwidth/latency tradeoffs for resource subscriptions."
}

  "text": "Envoy v1.31.2 implements comprehensive xDS (Extensible Discovery Service) API support for both State-of-the-World (SotW) and Delta protocol variants, both based on v3 proto definitions. While the codebase originally supported v2 (as evidenced by proto files at api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto and service/discovery/v2/), the runtime implementation exclusively uses v3 API types.\n\nCore Components:\n\n1. **ADS/gRPC Mux Implementations**: The primary ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service) implementation is in source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc for legacy SotW support and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc for the unified template-based implementation that handles both SotW and Delta variants. source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h/cc handles state-of-the-world protocol sessions\n   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h/cc handles delta protocol sessions\n   - Both inherit from BaseSubscriptionState template (subscription_state.h)\n\n3. **Proto Type Mapping and Selection**: source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.h/cc implements crucial functions that map resource type URLs to appropriate gRPC service methods:\n   - sotwGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects SotW endpoint for resource type\n   - deltaGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects Delta endpoint for resource type\n   - These function as the dual-version subscription handling mechanism, allowing Envoy to select between SotW and Delta protocols for each resource type\n\n4. **Subscription Factory Pattern**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.h/cc and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_collection_subscription_factory.h/cc implement factories that create appropriate subscription types:\n   - The dual-version subscription handling is achieved through protocol selection mechanisms (SotW vs Delta) rather than v2 vs v3 API versioning. Both protocols are v3-based and provide different bandwidth/latency tradeoffs for resource subscriptions."

Both use v3 proto types:\n   - SotW: envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest/DiscoveryResponse\n   - Delta: envoy::service::discovery::v3::DeltaDiscoveryRequest/DeltaDiscoveryResponse\n\n2. **Subscription State Management**: Separate implementations for protocol variants:\n   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h/cc handles state-of-the-world protocol sessions\n   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h/cc handles delta protocol sessions\n   - Both inherit from BaseSubscriptionState template (subscription_state.h)\n\n3. **Proto Type Mapping and Selection**: source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.h/cc implements crucial functions that map resource type URLs to appropriate gRPC service methods:\n   - sotwGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects SotW endpoint for resource type\n   - deltaGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects Delta endpoint for resource type\n   - These function as the dual-version subscription handling mechanism, allowing Envoy to select between SotW and Delta protocols for each resource type\n\n4. **Subscription Factory Pattern**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.h/cc and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_collection_subscription_factory.h/cc implement factories that create appropriate subscription types:\n   - GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates SotW subscriptions\n   - DeltaGrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates Delta subscriptions\n   - Selection between legacy mux (GrpcMuxImpl, NewGrpcMuxImpl) and unified mux (XdsMux::GrpcMux variants) controlled by runtime feature flag 'envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux'\n\n5. **Failover Support**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_failover.h provides arbitration between primary and failover config sources, supporting both SotW and Delta protocol variants through template specialization.\n\n6. **Resource Decoding**: source/common/config/decoded_resource_impl.h standardizes resource field access (version, resource, metadata) from v3 proto resources, regardless of protocol variant.\n\n7. **Proto Service Registration**: source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h ensures all v3 proto service descriptors (CDS, EDS, LDS, RDS, SRDS, RTDS, SDS, HDS, LEDS, ECDS) are available in the descriptor pool.\n\n8. **Watch and Resource Management**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h/cc manages resource subscriptions and version tracking, operating across both protocol variants.\n\nThe architecture demonstrates a sophisticated migration strategy where v2-to-v3 transition occurs at the proto definition level (with versioning annotations indicating previous_message_type), while the C++ runtime uses only v3 types. The dual-version subscription handling is achieved through protocol selection mechanisms (SotW vs Delta) rather than v2 vs v3 API versioning. Both protocols are v3-based and provide different bandwidth/latency tradeoffs for resource subscriptions."
}

1. **ADS/gRPC Mux Implementations**: The primary ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service) implementation is in source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc for legacy SotW support and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc for the unified template-based implementation that handles both SotW and Delta variants. Both use v3 proto types:
   - SotW: envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest/DiscoveryResponse
   - Delta: envoy::service::discovery::v3::DeltaDiscoveryRequest/DeltaDiscoveryResponse

2. **Subscription State Management**: Separate implementations for protocol variants:
   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h/cc handles state-of-the-world protocol sessions
   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h/cc handles delta protocol sessions
   - Both inherit from BaseSubscriptionState template (subscription_state.h)

3. **Proto Type Mapping and Selection**: source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.h/cc implements crucial functions that map resource type URLs to appropriate gRPC service methods:
   - sotwGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects SotW endpoint for resource type
   - deltaGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects Delta endpoint for resource type
   - These function as the dual-version subscription handling mechanism, allowing Envoy to select between SotW and Delta protocols for each resource type

4. **Subscription Factory Pattern**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.h/cc and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_collection_subscription_factory.h/cc implement factories that create appropriate subscription types:

6. **Resource Decoding**: source/common/config/decoded_resource_impl.h standardizes resource field access (version, resource, metadata) from v3 proto resources, regardless of protocol variant.

7. **Proto Service Registration**: source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h ensures all v3 proto service descriptors (CDS, EDS, LDS, RDS, SRDS, RTDS, SDS, HDS, LEDS, ECDS) are available in the descriptor pool.

8. **Watch and Resource Management**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h/cc manages resource subscriptions and version tracking, operating across both protocol variants.

The architecture demonstrates a sophisticated migration strategy where v2-to-v3 transition occurs at the proto definition level (with versioning annotations indicating previous_message_type), while the C++ runtime uses only v3 types. The dual-version subscription handling is achieved through protocol selection mechanisms (SotW vs Delta) rather than v2 vs v3 API versioning.     {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc", "symbol": "sotwGrpcMethod"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc", "symbol": "deltaGrpcMethod"}
  ],
  "text": "Envoy v1.31.2 implements comprehensive xDS (Extensible Discovery Service) API support for both State-of-the-World (SotW) and Delta protocol variants, both based on v3 proto definitions. While the codebase originally supported v2 (as evidenced by proto files at api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto and service/discovery/v2/), the runtime implementation exclusively uses v3 API types.\n\nCore Components:\n\n1. **ADS/gRPC Mux Implementations**: The primary ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service) implementation is in source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc for legacy SotW support and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc for the unified template-based implementation that handles both SotW and Delta variants. Both use v3 proto types:\n   - SotW: envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest/DiscoveryResponse\n   - Delta: envoy::service::discovery::v3::DeltaDiscoveryRequest/DeltaDiscoveryResponse\n\n2. **Subscription State Management**: Separate implementations for protocol variants:\n   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h/cc handles state-of-the-world protocol sessions\n   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h/cc handles delta protocol sessions\n   - Both inherit from BaseSubscriptionState template (subscription_state.h)\n\n3. **Proto Type Mapping and Selection**: source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.h/cc implements crucial functions that map resource type URLs to appropriate gRPC service methods:\n   - sotwGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects SotW endpoint for resource type\n   - deltaGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects Delta endpoint for resource type\n   - These function as the dual-version subscription handling mechanism, allowing Envoy to select between SotW and Delta protocols for each resource type\n\n4. **Subscription Factory Pattern**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.h/cc and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_collection_subscription_factory.h/cc implement factories that create appropriate subscription types:\n   - GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates SotW subscriptions\n   - DeltaGrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates Delta subscriptions\n   - Selection between legacy mux (GrpcMuxImpl, NewGrpcMuxImpl) and unified mux (XdsMux::GrpcMux variants) controlled by runtime feature flag 'envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux'\n\n5. **Failover Support**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_failover.h provides arbitration between primary and failover config sources, supporting both SotW and Delta protocol variants through template specialization.\n\n6. **Resource Decoding**: source/common/config/decoded_resource_impl.h standardizes resource field access (version, resource, metadata) from v3 proto resources, regardless of protocol variant.\n\n7. **Proto Service Registration**: source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h ensures all v3 proto service descriptors (CDS, EDS, LDS, RDS, SRDS, RTDS, SDS, HDS, LEDS, ECDS) are available in the descriptor pool.\n\n8. **Watch and Resource Management**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h/cc manages resource subscriptions and version tracking, operating across both protocol variants.\n\nThe architecture demonstrates a sophisticated migration strategy where v2-to-v3 transition occurs at the proto definition level (with versioning annotations indicating previous_message_type), while the C++ runtime uses only v3 types. The dual-version subscription handling is achieved through protocol selection mechanisms (SotW vs Delta) rather than v2 vs v3 API versioning. Both protocols are v3-based and provide different bandwidth/latency tradeoffs for resource subscriptions."
}

source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h/cc handles state-of-the-world protocol sessions\n   - source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h/cc handles delta protocol sessions\n   - GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates SotW subscriptions\n   - DeltaGrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates Delta subscriptions\n   - Selection between legacy mux (GrpcMuxImpl, NewGrpcMuxImpl) and unified mux (XdsMux::GrpcMux variants) controlled by runtime feature flag 'envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux'\n\n5. **Failover Support**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_failover.h provides arbitration between primary and failover config sources, supporting both SotW and Delta protocol variants through template specialization.\n\n6. **Resource Decoding**: source/common/config/decoded_resource_impl.h standardizes resource field access (version, resource, metadata) from v3 proto resources, regardless of protocol variant.\n\n7. **Proto Service Registration**: source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h ensures all v3 proto service descriptors (CDS, EDS, LDS, RDS, SRDS, RTDS, SDS, HDS, LEDS, ECDS) are available in the descriptor pool.\n\n8. **Watch and Resource Management**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h/cc manages resource subscriptions and version tracking, operating across both protocol variants.\n\nThe architecture demonstrates a sophisticated migration strategy where v2-to-v3 transition occurs at the proto definition level (with versioning annotations indicating previous_message_type), while the C++ runtime uses only v3 types. The dual-version subscription handling is achieved through protocol selection mechanisms (SotW vs Delta) rather than v2 vs v3 API versioning. Both protocols are v3-based and provide different bandwidth/latency tradeoffs for resource subscriptions."

While the codebase originally supported v2 (as evidenced by proto files at api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto and service/discovery/v2/), the runtime implementation exclusively uses v3 API types.\n\nCore Components:\n\n1. **ADS/gRPC Mux Implementations**: The primary ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service) implementation is in source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc for legacy SotW support and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc for the unified template-based implementation that handles both SotW and Delta variants. Both inherit from BaseSubscriptionState template (subscription_state.h)\n\n3. **Proto Type Mapping and Selection**: source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.h/cc implements crucial functions that map resource type URLs to appropriate gRPC service methods:\n   - sotwGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects SotW endpoint for resource type\n   - deltaGrpcMethod(type_url) - selects Delta endpoint for resource type\n   - These function as the dual-version subscription handling mechanism, allowing Envoy to select between SotW and Delta protocols for each resource type\n\n4. **Subscription Factory Pattern**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.h/cc and source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_collection_subscription_factory.h/cc implement factories that create appropriate subscription types:\n   - GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates SotW subscriptions\n   - DeltaGrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory - creates Delta subscriptions\n   - Selection between legacy mux (GrpcMuxImpl, NewGrpcMuxImpl) and unified mux (XdsMux::GrpcMux variants) controlled by runtime feature flag 'envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux'\n\n5. **Failover Support**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_failover.h provides arbitration between primary and failover config sources, supporting both SotW and Delta protocol variants through template specialization.\n\n6. **Resource Decoding**: source/common/config/decoded_resource_impl.h standardizes resource field access (version, resource, metadata) from v3 proto resources, regardless of protocol variant.\n\n7. **Proto Service Registration**: source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h ensures all v3 proto service descriptors (CDS, EDS, LDS, RDS, SRDS, RTDS, SDS, HDS, LEDS, ECDS) are available in the descriptor pool.\n\n8. **Watch and Resource Management**: source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h/cc manages resource subscriptions and version tracking, operating across both protocol variants.\n\nThe architecture demonstrates a sophisticated migration strategy where v2-to-v3 transition occurs at the proto definition level (with versioning annotations indicating previous_message_type), while the C++ runtime uses only v3 types. The dual-version subscription handling is achieved through protocol selection mechanisms (SotW vs Delta) rather than v2 vs v3 API versioning. steps.message:
Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the envoyproxy/envoy codebase (v1.31.2) to identify all C++ source files implementing xDS v2 and v3 API handling. 

## Summary

I found **52 source files** across the codebase that implement comprehensive xDS API support. The key finding is that while the codebase contains legacy v2 proto definitions, **the runtime implementation exclusively uses v3 proto types**. The "dual-version" handling refers to the two **protocol variants** (not API versions):

### Two Protocol Variants (Both v3):

1. **State-of-the-World (SotW)**: Traditional full-resource updates
   - Uses: `envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest/Response`

2. **Delta**: Bandwidth-optimized incremental updates  
   - Uses: `envoy::service::discovery::v3::DeltaDiscoveryRequest/Response`

### Core File Categories:

1. **ADS/gRPC Mux** (4 files): Legacy and unified template-based implementations
2. **Subscription State Management** (7 files): Separate SotW and Delta protocol handlers
3. **Proto Type Mapping** (2 files): Critical `sotwGrpcMethod()` and `deltaGrpcMethod()` functions that select between protocols
4. **Subscription Factories** (4 files): Create appropriate subscription types based on resource type
5. **Failover & Stream Management** (4 files): Multi-source arbitration with protocol support
6. **Resource Decoding** (8 files): Standardize field access across protocol variants
7. **Supporting Infrastructure** (23 files): Watch management, caching, queuing, utility functions

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file listings, key symbols, and a detailed narrative explaining the architecture.
